new_classes.label_en: grape must in fermentation or with fermentation arrested otherwise than by alcohol (CN 2204 30 10)
new_classes.label_de: Traubenmost, in Gärung befindlich oder ohne Alkohol stummgemacht (KN 2204 30 10)
new_classes.definition_en: Grape must of heading 2204 that is either still in the process of fermentation or whose fermentation has been arrested by means other than the addition of alcohol (e.g. by chilling, SO₂, or other physical/chemical means), corresponding to CN code 2204 30 10 and governed by Additional Notes 2(a), 2(b), 2(c) and 3 of Chapter 22.

A process quality indicating that the alcoholic fermentation of grape must has been stopped by means other than the addition of ethyl alcohol, such as chilling, sulfur dioxide, sterile filtration, or other physical or chemical agents.

coverage_explanation: The core distinguishing criterion — grape must that is either still in fermentation or has had fermentation arrested by means other than the addition of alcohol — is captured via the new class GrapeMust22043010 (a subclass of GrapeMust2204) and the FermentationArrestNonAlcohol quality restriction. The parent class GrapeMust2204 already anchors this within heading 2204. However, the legal text cross-references Additional Notes 2(a), 2(b), 2(c) and 3 of Chapter 22, which define quantitative thresholds (e.g. actual alcoholic strength and density ranges distinguishing grape must from wine). These note-defined numeric thresholds cannot be fully axiomatised here without introducing the specific note values (e.g. actual alcoholic strength ≤ 1% vol or density and fermentation-state constraints), which are not fully specified in the provided note text for this code alone. A partial coverage deduction is applied for those cross-referenced quantitative criteria.
